V-M-053.purpose: Verify the Phase-40 official MTProxy secret-sync reference and, for Phase-41, verify that it no longer drives active production issuance.

phase-41-supersession.item-1: Phase-41 production issuance uses KPprotoN fake-TLS SNI policy via M-043/M-044 instead of official secret manifests.
phase-41-supersession.item-2: M-053 may remain available only for rollback/reference behavior.

module-checks.check-1: cd backend && SECRET_KEY=test-secret-key-with-enough-length DATA_ENCRYPTION_KEY=dpFmfSVqfdAK3yx5MQV6Tcv35RZuw5MzaH0yhxJ01q0= PYTHONPATH=. ../.venv/bin/python -m pytest -o addopts='' tests/test_mtproxy_secret_sync.py tests/test_mtproto_provisioning.py tests/test_mtproto_api.py tests/test_mtproto_admin_ops.py tests/test_mtproto_assignment_registry.py tests/test_phase34_mtproto_integration.py tests/test_scheduler.py -v
module-checks.check-2: Run frontend and frontend-admin builds after payload/API shape changes.
module-checks.check-3: Run Phase-40 live smoke against one newly verified user and one revoked/reissued user without printing secrets.
module-checks.check-4: Verify active issuance does not render or apply official secret manifests after KPprotoN rollback.

scenarios.scenario-1: Verified user receives one canonical official credential-mode assignment and owner-only secure link with secret=dd<32-hex-secret>.
scenarios.scenario-2: Repeated verified-registration or dashboard refresh reuses the same active assignment unless explicit reissue is requested.
scenarios.scenario-3: Active assignments render to a deterministic redacted manifest and apply only when the manifest fingerprint changes.
scenarios.scenario-4: Admin revoke removes exactly one user secret and preserves unrelated active users.
scenarios.scenario-5: Admin reissue rotates exactly one user secret, invalidates the old runtime secret, and returns the new owner payload only to the owner.
scenarios.scenario-6: Startup replay after backend, runtime, or restored-server startup restores all active assignment secrets.
scenarios.scenario-7: Unverified user receives no MTProxy secret and no runtime apply attempt.
scenarios.scenario-8: Runtime apply failure returns degraded state, retries safely, and does not block normal KrotPN login.
scenarios.scenario-9: Oversized manifest or command-line guard prevents unsafe runtime launch and reports scale-out requirement.
scenarios.scenario-10: Phase-41 reissue creates a KPprotoN fake-TLS assignment payload and bypasses official manifest sync.

redaction-rules.rule-1: Admin APIs, audit events, health checks, task logs, and smoke output may expose only assignment id, user id when authorized, masked hostname, secret fingerprint, count, status, and timestamps.
redaction-rules.rule-2: Raw secret is allowed only in the authenticated owner payload needed to configure Telegram and must not be persisted in admin-visible fields.

evidence-levels.level-1: Deterministic unit assertions first: secret format, stable derivation or encrypted persistence contract, owner-only payload, idempotency, redaction, and lifecycle transitions.
evidence-levels.level-2: Trace assertions second: required log markers on create, apply, revoke, reissue, replay, and degraded branches.
evidence-levels.level-3: Integration checks third: scheduler replay, runtime adapter calls, API/admin responses, and UI payload rendering.

failure-policy.policy-1: Any raw secret in admin responses, audit events, logs, task output, or smoke output fails verification.
failure-policy.policy-2: Any revoke or reissue operation affecting more than the target assignment fails verification.
failure-policy.policy-3: Any MTProxy issuance before email verification fails verification.
failure-policy.policy-4: Any active user payload returned before runtime apply succeeds or explicitly reports degraded state fails verification.
failure-policy.policy-5: After Phase-41 execution, any official manifest apply during normal user issuance fails verification unless rollback is explicit.

evidence.evidence-1: 2026-05-19: Phase-41 verification design marks official secret sync as superseded for production and requires active issuance to use KPprotoN fake-TLS policy instead.
evidence.evidence-2: 2026-05-19: Phase-41 local execution verified active provisioning, admin actions, and scheduler replay bypass official manifest sync in favor of M-044 KPprotoN policy apply.
